title: Thelypteris falcatipinnula

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris falcatipinnula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris falcatipinnula is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in the production of extracts and capsules for its potential health benefits.

This family is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes a wide range of ferns. The genus Thelypteris includes about 200 species of ferns, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions.
